input.background: Let us return to the birth weight illustrative data concerning 10 SIDS cases. We have established that \(\bar{x}=2890.5\) grams and \(s=720.0\). Confidence intervals for \(\mu\) at \(90 \%, 95 \%\), and \(99 \%\) levels of confidence will be calculated.
input.question: Calculate the \(90\%\) confidence interval for \(\mu\).

output.answer: For \(90\%\) confidence, use \(t_{9,0.95}=1.833\) [Table C], so the \(90\%\) confidence interval for \(\mu\) is \(\bar{x} \pm \left(t_{n-1,1-\xi}\right)\left(SE_{\bar{x}}\right)=2890.5 \pm (1.833)(227.684)=2890.5 \pm 417.3=(2473.2\) to \(3307.8\)) grams.
